# Context Map

This project produces a chess engine that, given a board position (FEN), returns a move played in the style of a specific named human player chosen from a closed roster. Work splits into three contexts.

## Contexts

- [Training](./src/training/CONTEXT.md) — offline pipeline: ingests Game archives, trains the Base model on a broad corpus, and fine-tunes one Adapter per Roster Player.
- [Engine](./src/engine/CONTEXT.md) — runtime server: loads the Base model + all Roster Adapters at startup, accepts a Position + Opponent choice, returns a legal Move. Single-worker, serialized GPU forward.
- [Web](./src/web/CONTEXT.md) — browser-facing UI: lets the user pick an Opponent (Roster Player), play a Session against the resulting Adapter, and see Stockfish's recommendation alongside the Adapter's Move.

## Relationships

- **Training → Engine**: Training emits two artifact families consumed by Engine — the Base model checkpoint and the per-Player Adapter weights. Adapters are produced once per Roster member at dev-time; Engine loads them at runtime.
- **Engine ↔ Web**: Web sends the current Position (FEN) plus the selected Opponent's `adapter_id` plus the Session's `move_history`; Engine returns a single legal Move (and optionally the policy distribution over candidates). Synchronous request/response, no events. Web owns Session storage; Engine is stateless per request.
- **Web ↔ Stockfish (in-browser)**: Web runs `stockfish.wasm` in a Web Worker to produce a recommended Move for the comparison panel. Engine is not in the Stockfish path; the two are independent move sources rendered side by side.

# Merge gate — quick reference

This project runs the **merge-gate** CI workflow on every pull
request. The gate is the pre-merge phase of the harness; the full
operations playbook (canary suite, soft→hard promotion, validator
disagreement runbook, roll-back) lives at
[`docs/merge-gate-operations.md`](merge-gate-operations.md).

## Blocking rule

`critical` or `high` severity Codex findings classified `uphold` by
the Claude validator block merge. `medium` / `low` findings are
commented but never block. See ADR-0005 for why this MVP runs the
Claude validator only (Gemini concordance is deferred pending
soft-mode measurement data).

## Severity matrix

| Severity | Validator `uphold` | Validator `dismiss` (cited) | Validator `unsure` |
|---|---|---|---|
| critical | **block**    | pass         | **block** (fail-safe) |
| high     | **block**    | pass         | **block** (fail-safe) |
| medium   | comment only | comment only | comment only          |
| low      | comment only | comment only | comment only          |

## Bypass label

Apply the `merge-gate-bypass` label to a PR to skip the gate. Bypass
is the **audited bypass lane** — one human (not the PR author) must
apply the label, leave a PR comment with the reason, and add an
entry to the plan repo's bypass log within one business day. See
operations playbook §4 for the full bypass policy.

## Soft → Hard promotion

The gate ships in **soft mode** (`harness.toml`
`[merge-gate].soft_mode_default = "true"`): runs, reports, never
blocks. Flip to hard mode after meeting the quantitative gates in
operations playbook §2 (N ≥ 10 PRs, FPR ≤ 1, human sign-off) **and**
enabling the branch-protection rule on the check
`merge-gate / codex-review`.

## Local re-installation

The skill `/setup-merge-gate` is idempotent. Re-running it shows a
diff for the `[merge-gate]` section of `harness.toml` and prompts
once; vendored agent and skill files under `.claude/` are overwritten
unconditionally. `--uninstall` removes the workflow, the
`[merge-gate]` section, the vendored copies, and this document (only
if its first-line marker is intact).

+# Canary 09 — Dim B data-loss: missing chess.js undo() in engine-error rollback
+
+> **DO NOT MERGE.** This PR is a merge-gate canary; the branch
+> exists solely to observe how the gate responds to a deliberate
+> defect injection. See claude-harness-work#23 Dimension B.
+
+## Path under test
+
+`block` — Codex grades the defect `high` or `critical` (missing
+rollback / state corruption), validator upholds citing the
+Invariant-5 comment block, gate blocks under hard mode
+(`critical|high ∩ uphold ≥ 1`).
+
+Category: **data-loss** (Dim B #1). Phase 2 surface exposes
+this category through `useChessSession.ts:74–87` — the
+engine-error rollback block calls `chessRef.current.undo()`
+explicitly to keep chess.js, React state, and history in sync.
+
+## Injected defect
+
+- **File touched**: `src/web/app/hooks/useChessSession.ts`
+- **Line(s)**: removed line 82 (was `chessRef.current.undo()`).
+- **What changed**: a single-line deletion. The engine-error
+  branch in `requestEngineMove` no longer rolls back chess.js
+  state. `setFen(chessRef.current.fen())` still runs, but now
+  observes the *unrolled* internal chess.js board, so the
+  user's just-attempted move is committed to chess.js while
+  React `history` is sliced back by one and `error` is set.
+  Three pieces of state diverge.
+- **Why it's a defect (the case for `uphold`)**: The block sits
+  directly under the comment naming "Invariant 5" / 422
+  defensive freeze. The whole block exists *to* roll back when
+  the server rejects the user's move. After the deletion:
+  - chess.js retains the user's move.
+  - React `fen` reflects that retained-move position.
+  - `history` loses one entry (slice -1).
+  - `awaiting` clears, `error` is set.
+  Next user move proceeds against a position the server never
+  agreed to — and chess.js's notion of legal moves now differs
+  from the server's. Classic missing-rollback data-loss /
+  state-corruption defect.
+
+## Expected outcome
+
+- **Codex severity**: `high` or `critical`
+- **Codex finding count (high+critical)**: `≥ 1`
+- **Validator verdict per finding**: `uphold` (Invariant 5
+  comment block is local, explicit, and unambiguous; the
+  whole branch is *named* "rollback")
+- **Gate conclusion**: `block` — sticky should say the gate
+  would block under hard mode
+- **Sticky comment top line should read**: needs-attention / block
+
+## What this attempt measures
+
+The `block` path has already been verified end-to-end (Dim A
+C1 in `01.md`). #09 re-exercises it on a different *kind* of
+defect — Phase 2 frontend / state-coherence rather than
+Phase 1 hardcoded secret — to confirm the same path fires for
+Dim B's data-loss category.
+
+The bar is **category coverage**, not exact severity prediction.
+A `high`-graded finding upheld and blocked is success; so is
+`critical`. A `medium` upheld → comment-only would be a
+near-miss (severity-floor active) but **not** a category miss:
+the data-loss category was still observed end-to-end.
+
+A real miss would be: Codex returns 0 findings (no flag at all)
+or grades the defect non-data-loss in character (e.g. labels
+it cosmetic UI). In that case, propose a second data-loss
+injection per the ≥2-attempts rule.
+
+## Run record
+
+| Run | Commit | GH Actions run ID | Codex severity | Validator verdict | Sticky verdict | Notes |
+|-----|--------|---|---|---|---|---|
+| 1   |        |   |   |   |   |       |
+
+Artefacts at `.scratch/merge-gate-canary/09-artefacts/run-1/`.
+
+## Actual vs expected
+
+(filled in after the gate completes)
+
+## Closing
+
+- [ ] PR labelled `canary` on chess_transformer
+- [ ] PR closed without merge
+- [ ] Branch deleted (`gh pr close --delete-branch`)
+- [ ] Brief committed to `.scratch/merge-gate-canary/09.md`
+- [ ] Artefacts committed under `09-artefacts/`

# Web

The browser-facing UI: lets the user choose a Roster Player, play a complete chess Session against the resulting Adapter, and see Stockfish's recommendation alongside the Adapter's chosen Move.

## Language

**Session**:
One complete chess play instance in a single browser tab — from the starting Position to a Terminal state (or to a new-Session reset). Bounded by: one human player + one chosen Adapter (the Opponent), one selected human color (White or Black), one continuous game-state owned by the browser's `chess.js`. Distinct from Training's **Game** — a training **Game** is an archived sequence with one Player on each side; a Session is live, has only one Player (the Opponent), and is never persisted server-side. The same Position with the same Opponent and the same `move_history` returns the same Move under greedy decoding; under sampling, varied.
_Avoid_: bare "Game" when meaning live play (use **Session**); "match" (reserved for self-play matches during Evaluation, see Training CONTEXT).

**Opponent**:
The Roster **Player** selected for the current Session, exposed to the user as "Playing against: Magnus Carlsen". Realized in the wire as the `adapter_id` sent on every `/move` request. From the user's perspective the Opponent is "the Player I face"; from the Engine's perspective it is "which Adapter is active for this request". Switching Opponents during a Session is not supported in v1 — selection is made at Session start and persists until a new Session.
_Avoid_: bare "Player" in Web context (Player is Roster-only; the user is never a Player).

**Terminal state**:
The set of conditions on which a Session ends. Five chess-rule terminations are detected and rendered by the Web client via `chess.js` (the Engine is not consulted — see Engine CONTEXT for the "is the Session over?" non-contract):
1. **Checkmate** — side to move has no legal Moves and is in check.
2. **Stalemate** — side to move has no legal Moves and is not in check.
3. **Threefold repetition** — same Position occurs three times (FIDE-canonical equality; `chess.js` `isThreefoldRepetition()`). User-claimable per FIDE; in v1 the Web client auto-ends the Session.
4. **50-move rule** — 100 half-moves without capture or pawn move. User-claimable per FIDE; auto-ended as above.
5. **Insufficient material** — neither side has mating material per FIDE.

In addition, the user can **resign** or start a **new Session** at any point — user-initiated terminations, not chess-rule terminations.
_Avoid_: "game-over event" — **Terminal state** is the canonical term, and it is the enum-shaped concept the UI dispatches on.

## Relationships

- A **Session** is constituted by one **Opponent** (an Adapter), one human color choice, and a growing `move_history` (the same UCI list the Engine receives on every `/move` request). When a **Terminal state** is reached, the Session ends.
- **Web ↔ Engine**: see `src/engine/CONTEXT.md`. Web sends Position + `adapter_id` + `move_history` per turn; Engine returns one legal Move. Web owns Session storage; the Engine is stateless per request.
- **Web ↔ Stockfish (in-browser)**: `stockfish.wasm` runs in a Web Worker and analyses the current Position for the comparison panel. Stockfish never sees the Opponent's identity or the Adapter — it is an independent analysis source rendered side by side with the Adapter's Move.

## Implementation notes

Not domain language, but load-bearing v1 decisions:

- Session state lives **only in the browser**. Page refresh discards it. No server-side persistence — see PRD Out of Scope on multiplayer/accounts.
- A user has exactly **one Opponent per Session**. Changing Opponent requires a new Session.
- Comparison panel, Player selector, move list, new-Session button are UI components (implementation-incidental) and are deliberately kept out of this glossary.

from __future__ import annotations

import chess
import pytest
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

from playlike_engine.api import app

STARTING_FEN = chess.STARTING_FEN
MID_GAME_FEN = "r1bqkb1r/pppp1ppp/2n2n2/4p3/4P3/2N2N2/PPPP1PPP/R1BQKB1R w KQkq - 4 4"
CHECKMATE_FEN = "rnb1kbnr/pppp1ppp/8/4p3/6Pq/5P2/PPPPP2P/RNBQKBNR w KQkq - 1 3"
INVALID_FEN_STR = "not a fen"


@pytest.fixture(scope="module")
def client() -> TestClient:
    return TestClient(app)


def test_starting_position_returns_legal_uci(client: TestClient) -> None:
    response = client.post("/move", json={"fen": STARTING_FEN})
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert set(body.keys()) == {"move_uci"}
    board = chess.Board(STARTING_FEN)
    assert chess.Move.from_uci(body["move_uci"]) in board.legal_moves


def test_mid_game_returns_legal_uci(client: TestClient) -> None:
    response = client.post("/move", json={"fen": MID_GAME_FEN})
    assert response.status_code == 200
    board = chess.Board(MID_GAME_FEN)
    assert chess.Move.from_uci(response.json()["move_uci"]) in board.legal_moves


def test_invalid_fen_returns_422(client: TestClient) -> None:
    response = client.post("/move", json={"fen": INVALID_FEN_STR})
    assert response.status_code == 422


def test_checkmate_fen_returns_422(client: TestClient) -> None:
    response = client.post("/move", json={"fen": CHECKMATE_FEN})
    assert response.status_code == 422


def test_extra_field_rejected_by_strict_schema(client: TestClient) -> None:
    response = client.post(
        "/move",
        json={"fen": STARTING_FEN, "adapter_id": "magnus"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 422

from __future__ import annotations

import chess
import pytest
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient

from playlike_engine.api import app

STARTING_FEN = chess.STARTING_FEN
MID_GAME_FEN = "r1bqkb1r/pppp1ppp/2n2n2/4p3/4P3/2N2N2/PPPP1PPP/R1BQKB1R w KQkq - 4 4"
CHECKMATE_FEN = "rnb1kbnr/pppp1ppp/8/4p3/6Pq/5P2/PPPPP2P/RNBQKBNR w KQkq - 1 3"
INVALID_FEN_STR = "not a fen"


@pytest.fixture(scope="module")
def client() -> TestClient:
    return TestClient(app)


def test_starting_position_returns_legal_uci(client: TestClient) -> None:
    response = client.post("/move", json={"fen": STARTING_FEN})
    assert response.status_code == 200
    body = response.json()
    assert set(body.keys()) == {"move_uci"}
    board = chess.Board(STARTING_FEN)
    assert chess.Move.from_uci(body["move_uci"]) in board.legal_moves


def test_mid_game_returns_legal_uci(client: TestClient) -> None:
    response = client.post("/move", json={"fen": MID_GAME_FEN})
    assert response.status_code == 200
    board = chess.Board(MID_GAME_FEN)
    assert chess.Move.from_uci(response.json()["move_uci"]) in board.legal_moves


def test_invalid_fen_returns_422(client: TestClient) -> None:
    response = client.post("/move", json={"fen": INVALID_FEN_STR})
    assert response.status_code == 422


def test_checkmate_fen_returns_422(client: TestClient) -> None:
    response = client.post("/move", json={"fen": CHECKMATE_FEN})
    assert response.status_code == 422


def test_extra_field_rejected_by_strict_schema(client: TestClient) -> None:
    response = client.post(
        "/move",
        json={"fen": STARTING_FEN, "adapter_id": "magnus"},
    )
    assert response.status_code == 422

import chess

# Merge gate — operations playbook

This document is the human-facing playbook for the **Merge gate** —
the **Gate** for the pre-merge phase of the **Harness** (see
`CONTEXT-MAP.md`). The gate runs Codex's adversarial review as the
primary reviewer and a Claude validator (MVP) that classifies each
finding against project context before the gate decides to block.
The MVP runs a single Claude validator per
[ADR-0005](adr/0005-claude-only-validator-mvp-gemini-deferred.md);
a second validator returns once issue #10's soft-mode measurement
data is in.

The **Setup skill** (`/setup-merge-gate`) installs the gate's
artifacts; it is not itself a gate. This playbook describes how to
*operate* the installed gate.

Glossary terms (`Harness`, `Gate`, `Audited bypass lane`,
`Oracle weakening`) are defined in `CONTEXT-MAP.md` — do not redefine
them here; link or quote.

---

## 1. Operational phases at a glance

| Phase | Branch protection | Gate behaviour | Typical use |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Soft** | Not required | Runs, posts findings, **never blocks** | Data-collection — every PR is a measurement |
| **Hard** | Required check | Blocks merge on `critical` or `high` ∩ `uphold` | Steady state — gate is trusted |

Mode is recorded in `harness.toml` under `[merge-gate].soft_mode_default`
(string boolean: `"true"` = soft, `"false"` = hard). The flip from
`"true"` to `"false"` is HITL and is governed by §2 below.

The gate's check name is stable — `merge-gate / codex-review` — so
branch-protection references do not need updating across mode flips.

---

## 2. Soft → Hard promotion checklist

The flip from `soft` to `hard` is the single moment the gate's
authority changes. Treat it as a release, not a tweak. All items
must be checked, and the human sign-off line must be filled in.

### Quantitative gates

- [ ] **N ≥ 10 non-canary PRs** measured under soft mode (canary
      PRs are excluded — they are deliberate defect injections, not
      organic traffic).
- [ ] **False-positive rate ≤ 1** across the N=10 set, where a false
      positive is *validator said block, human said "I would not have
      wanted this blocked"*.
- [ ] **False-negative rate recorded** — *validator said pass, human
      said "I would have wanted this blocked"*. A high FNR is **not**
      a blocker for the soft→hard flip (the gate's purpose is to
      cut false negatives over time), but it must be written down so
      the next iteration can target it.

### Data layout

Each PR's soft-mode data lives in the project's plan repo under
`.scratch/merge-gate/runs/<pr-number>.md` and records:

- Codex JSON output (artefact link or inline)
- Claude verdict
- Final decision the gate would have made
- Human verdict: would I have wanted this blocked? (yes / no / unsure)
- One-line rationale if validator and human diverged

A second validator's verdict slot will be added to this layout when
issue #10's measurement motivates re-introducing it.

### Qualitative review

- [ ] Skim every entry. Look for *patterns* in the false positives
      (e.g. "Codex keeps flagging our intentional retry-without-cap
      because AGENTS.md does not state the upstream timeout
      contract") rather than treating each as isolated noise. A
      pattern is a signal to tighten the validator's project
      references (§5), not to lower the bar.
- [ ] Confirm the **Audited bypass lane** was never silently used in
      this window — every `merge-gate-bypass` label application
      should appear in §4's audit log.

### Human sign-off

> Signed off by: `<name>`
> Date: `<YYYY-MM-DD>`
> N = `<count>` PRs · FPR = `<count>/<N>`
> Flipping `[merge-gate].soft_mode_default` from `"true"` to `"false"` and enabling the
> branch-protection rule on `merge-gate / codex-review`.

### After the flip

- Update `harness.toml`: `[merge-gate].soft_mode_default = "false"`.
- Enable the branch-protection rule (the `/setup-merge-gate` install
  output printed the exact rule text — keep it in the install log).
- Announce the flip in whatever channel the team uses; the first
  organic block under hard mode is the moment people learn the gate
  is real.

If any quantitative gate is unmet: **do not flip**. Open or update
the soft→hard tracking issue with what is missing and which upstream
issue is the fix path (tighten validator prompt, expand AGENTS.md
context, etc.).

---

## 3. Canary-PR guide

Canary PRs are deliberate, scoped defect injections on throwaway
branches — they exercise the gate against synthetic defects in known
risk categories, so the gate's behaviour can be measured against a
known answer key.

### What to canary

At minimum, one PR per category Codex's adversarial-review prompt
prioritises:

| Category | Example defect | Expected severity | Expected verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| auth / authz boundary | Drop a role check on an admin endpoint | `critical` | `should-block-critical` |
| silent data loss / missing rollback | Remove a transaction wrapper around a multi-write path | `high` | `should-block-critical` |
| race / version-skew | Drop an optimistic-lock check on a counter update | `high` | `should-block-critical` |
| observability gap | Replace a structured error log with a swallowed exception | `medium` | `should-warn-medium` |

`should-block-critical` means the gate's final decision is *block*
under hard mode. `should-warn-medium` means the gate comments but
does not block — `medium` and `low` severities are always
non-blocking by the gate's blocking rule.

### Operating canaries

- Each canary's brief lives at
  `.scratch/merge-gate-canary/<NN>.md` and states injected defect,
  category, expected severity, expected validator verdict.
- Canary branches are pushed and PRs opened as **draft**.
- Canary PRs are **labelled `canary`** so they are excluded from the
  soft-mode FPR measurement in §2 (they are not organic traffic).
- Canary branches are **closed, never merged**.

### Interpreting a Codex false positive on a canary

A "Codex false positive" on a canary means Codex flagged something
the brief did *not* inject. Two common causes:

1. **Real latent defect.** The diff genuinely contains a problem
   unrelated to the injection. Record it as a found bug in the
   canary brief — that is a useful side effect, not noise.
2. **Context starvation.** Codex flagged code that looks risky in
   isolation but is safe given project conventions that AGENTS.md
   does not yet spell out. This is the validator's job to dismiss
   with a citation (γ Strict — see §5). If the validator also fails
   to dismiss, treat it as a *project-context gap*: open an issue per
   §4 tagged `merge-gate-fp` with a pointer to the AGENTS.md /
   `CONTEXT-MAP.md` text that should have made the dismissal possible.

### Interpreting a missed canary

If a canary's expected severity is `critical` but Codex returns
`medium`, or returns nothing, record it in the brief with a short
hypothesis (e.g. "Codex missed the rollback case — likely because
the diff did not include the migration file"). Missed canaries are
the input for tightening the canary suite and/or the Codex prompt
in a later iteration — they are not blockers for soft→hard.

---

## 4. Audited bypass lane — the `merge-gate-bypass` label

The merge gate provides an **Audited bypass lane** in the sense
defined in `CONTEXT-MAP.md`: an acknowledged, recorded way to pass the
gate without satisfying it. The lane exists so emergencies are not
forced through the silent-circumvention door. It is **not** a
shortcut for routine merges.

### When the label is allowed

- **Production incident hotfix** where the cost of waiting for the
  gate exceeds the cost of a missed finding, and the fix is small
  enough for a human to review end-to-end.
- **Confirmed Codex false positive** that the validator could not
  dismiss with a citation, where the team has already opened a
  `merge-gate-fp` issue per §5 and is unwilling to block the PR on
  the fix.
- **Infrastructure / docs-only PR** that should have hit the
  `docs-only` path glob but did not (treat the missed glob as a
  bug — fix the workflow's glob in a follow-up).

The label is **never** appropriate for:

- "The gate is slow today" — that is a §7 cost/latency issue, file
  it there.
- "I disagree with the finding" without a written argument — open a
  `merge-gate-fp` issue and resolve through §5, not the bypass lane.
- Avoiding the validator `unsure` runbook in §6 — bypass is the
  lane of last resort, not the default for `unsure`.

### Approval

- **Single approver model.** One human who is not the PR author must
  apply the `merge-gate-bypass` label and leave a PR comment with
  the reason (one of the three categories above) and a link to any
  follow-up issue.
- For production-incident hotfixes, the approver is the on-call;
  for the other two categories, the approver is a project
  maintainer.

### Audit trail

- The label application is itself a GitHub event; that is the
  primary audit record.
- The approver's PR comment is the human-readable rationale.
- A follow-up entry is added to
  `.scratch/merge-gate/bypass-log.md` in the plan repo within one
  business day: PR link, approver, category, follow-up issue link.

The finding holds up against the code as written.\\n\\n[LOW] uphold src/engine/playlike_engine/api.py:41 — src/engine/playlike_engine/api.py:42: log.info(\\\"engine session token: %s\\\", token) — token never included in MoveResponse or response headers\\n\\nblock_count: 0\\nbypass_eligible: yes\\naction: merge ok\\n\",\n.scratch/merge-gate-canary/03-artefacts/run-1/codex-review.json:18:{\"type\":\"item.completed\",\"item\":{\"id\":\"item_8\",\"type\":\"command_execution\",\"command\":\"/bin/bash -lc \\\"sed -n '1,220p' docs/merge-gate-operations.md\\\"\",\"aggregated_output\":\"<!-- generated by /setup-merge-gate v1; do not edit by hand -->\\n\\n# Merge gate — operations playbook\\n\\nThis document is the human-facing playbook for the **Merge gate** —\\nthe **Gate** for the pre-merge phase of the **Harness** (see\\n`CONTEXT-MAP.md`). The gate runs Codex's adversarial review as the\\nprimary reviewer and a Claude validator (MVP) that classifies each\\nfinding against project context before the gate decides to block.\\nThe MVP runs a single Claude validator per\\n[ADR-0005](adr/0005-claude-only-validator-mvp-gemini-deferred.md);\\na second validator returns once issue #10's soft-mode measurement\\ndata is in.\\n\\nThe **Setup skill** (`/setup-merge-gate`) installs the gate's\\nartifacts; it is not itself a gate. This playbook describes how to\\n*operate* the installed gate.\\n\\nGlossary terms (`Harness`, `Gate`, `Audited bypass lane`,\\n`Oracle weakening`) are defined in `CONTEXT-MAP.md` — do not redefine\\nthem here; link or quote.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. Operational phases at a glance\\n\\n| Phase | Branch protection | Gate behaviour | Typical use |\\n|---|---|---|---|\\n| **Soft** | Not required | Runs, posts findings, **never blocks** | Data-collection — every PR is a measurement |\\n| **Hard** | Required check | Blocks merge on `critical` or `high` ∩ `uphold` | Steady state — gate is trusted |\\n\\nMode is recorded in `harness.toml` under `[merge-gate].soft_mode_default`\\n(string boolean: `\\\"true\\\"` = soft, `\\\"false\\\"` = hard). The flip from\\n`\\\"true\\\"` to `\\\"false\\\"` is HITL and is governed by §2 below.\\n\\nThe gate's check name is stable — `merge-gate / codex-review` — so\\nbranch-protection references do not need updating across mode flips.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. Soft → Hard promotion checklist\\n\\nThe flip from `soft` to `hard` is the single moment the gate's\\nauthority changes. Treat it as a release, not a tweak. All items\\nmust be checked, and the human sign-off line must be filled in.\\n\\n### Quantitative gates\\n\\n- [ ] **N ≥ 10 non-canary PRs** measured under soft mode (canary\\n      PRs are excluded — they are deliberate defect injections, not\\n      organic traffic).\\n- [ ] **False-positive rate ≤ 1** across the N=10 set, where a false\\n      positive is *validator said block, human said \\\"I would not have\\n      wanted this blocked\\\"*.\\n- [ ] **False-negative rate recorded** — *validator said pass, human\\n      said \\\"I would have wanted this blocked\\\"*. A high FNR is **not**\\n      a blocker for the soft→hard flip (the gate's purpose is to\\n      cut false negatives over time), but it must be written down so\\n      the next iteration can target it.\\n\\n### Data layout\\n\\nEach PR's soft-mode data lives in the project's plan repo under\\n`.scratch/merge-gate/runs/<pr-number>.md` and records:\\n\\n- Codex JSON output (artefact link or inline)\\n- Claude verdict\\n- Final decision the gate would have made\\n- Human verdict: would I have wanted this blocked? (yes / no / unsure)\\n- One-line rationale if validator and human diverged\\n\\nA second validator's verdict slot will be added to this layout when\\nissue #10's measurement motivates re-introducing it.\\n\\n### Qualitative review\\n\\n- [ ] Skim every entry. Look for *patterns* in the false positives\\n      (e.g. \\\"Codex keeps flagging our intentional retry-without-cap\\n      because AGENTS.md does not state the upstream timeout\\n      contract\\\") rather than treating each as isolated noise. A\\n      pattern is a signal to tighten the validator's project\\n      references (§5), not to lower the bar.\\n- [ ] Confirm the **Audited bypass lane** was never silently used in\\n      this window — every `merge-gate-bypass` label application\\n      should appear in §4's audit log.\\n\\n### Human sign-off\\n\\n> Signed off by: `<name>`\\n> Date: `<YYYY-MM-DD>`\\n> N = `<count>` PRs · FPR = `<count>/<N>`\\n> Flipping `[merge-gate].soft_mode_default` from `\\\"true\\\"` to `\\\"false\\\"` and enabling the\\n> branch-protection rule on `merge-gate / codex-review`.\\n\\n### After the flip\\n\\n- Update `harness.toml`: `[merge-gate].soft_mode_default = \\\"false\\\"`.\\n- Enable the branch-protection rule (the `/setup-merge-gate` install\\n  output printed the exact rule text — keep it in the install log).\\n- Announce the flip in whatever channel the team uses; the first\\n  organic block under hard mode is the moment people learn the gate\\n  is real.\\n\\nIf any quantitative gate is unmet: **do not flip**. Open or update\\nthe soft→hard tracking issue with what is missing and which upstream\\nissue is the fix path (tighten validator prompt, expand AGENTS.md\\ncontext, etc.).\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. Canary-PR guide\\n\\nCanary PRs are deliberate, scoped defect injections on throwaway\\nbranches — they exercise the gate against synthetic defects in known\\nrisk categories, so the gate's behaviour can be measured against a\\nknown answer key.\\n\\n### What to canary\\n\\nAt minimum, one PR per category Codex's adversarial-review prompt\\nprioritises:\\n\\n| Category | Example defect | Expected severity | Expected verdict |\\n|---|---|---|---|\\n| auth / authz boundary | Drop a role check on an admin endpoint | `critical` | `should-block-critical` |\\n| silent data loss / missing rollback | Remove a transaction wrapper around a multi-write path | `high` | `should-block-critical` |\\n| race / version-skew | Drop an optimistic-lock check on a counter update | `high` | `should-block-critical` |\\n| observability gap | Replace a structured error log with a swallowed exception | `medium` | `should-warn-medium` |\\n\\n`should-block-critical` means the gate's final decision is *block*\\nunder hard mode. `should-warn-medium` means the gate comments but\\ndoes not block — `medium` and `low` severities are always\\nnon-blocking by the gate's blocking rule.\\n\\n### Operating canaries\\n\\n- Each canary's brief lives at\\n  `.scratch/merge-gate-canary/<NN>.md` and states injected defect,\\n  category, expected severity, expected validator verdict.\\n- Canary branches are pushed and PRs opened as **draft**.\\n- Canary PRs are **labelled `canary`** so they are excluded from the\\n  soft-mode FPR measurement in §2 (they are not organic traffic).\\n- Canary branches are **closed, never merged**.\\n\\n### Interpreting a Codex false positive on a canary\\n\\nA \\\"Codex false positive\\\" on a canary means Codex flagged something\\nthe brief did *not* inject. Two common causes:\\n\\n1. **Real latent defect.** The diff genuinely contains a problem\\n   unrelated to the injection. Record it as a found bug in the\\n   canary brief — that is a useful side effect, not noise.\\n2. **Context starvation.** Codex flagged code that looks risky in\\n   isolation but is safe given project conventions that AGENTS.md\\n   does not yet spell out. This is the validator's job to dismiss\\n   with a citation (γ Strict — see §5). Delegating to `python-chess` is deliberate — FIDE's \\\\\\\"same Position\\\\\\\" definition has subtle ep/castling/clock semantics that are easy to get wrong with manual FEN equality. The model is Markov over FEN, so without this stage the Inference Engine would have no protection against accidentally drawing a winning Position by repeating it (see ADR-0001 Markov section).\\\\n\\\\nThe 50-move-rule clock lives in the FEN and is already handled by stage 1. 5-fold and 75-move (automatic) are detected client-side as game-over events; the Inference Engine itself does not refuse to move once they occur.\\\\n\\\\n_Avoid_: \\\\\\\"legal moves filter\\\\\\\" — **mask** is the canonical term.\\\\n\\\\n**Policy distribution**:\\\\nThe post-mask, post-softmax distribution over the 1968 UCI actions for a given Position+Adapter+decoding-mode. Returned optionally for the comparison panel and the Evaluation Harness.\\\\n\\\\n## Implementation notes\\\\n\\\\nNot domain language, but load-bearing v1 decisions:\\\\n\\\\n- The Inference Engine runs as a **single FastAPI worker process** on one GPU. Base + 5 Adapters share one VRAM allocation (≈52MB in FP16). All `/move` requests serialize through a GPU forward lock — concurrent requests are queued, not parallel.\\\\n- Adapter selection is **in-place per request**: the worker activates the requested Adapter, runs the forward pass, returns the Move, then accepts the next request. Two simultaneous requests for different Adapters are safe because they cannot overlap on the GPU (the lock guarantees no interleaved state mutation).\\\\n- The server is **stateless per request**. Game state lives in the browser (`chess.js`). The same Position with the same Adapter under greedy decoding returns the same Move every time; under sampling, varied.\\\\n- Throughput ceiling ≈ 7–10 moves/sec on the 3080. Engine never re-trains or modifies these artifacts.\\\\n- **Engine ↔ Web**: Web calls the Engine via `POST /move` (FEN + adapter_id + move_history → Move) and `WebSocket /play`. Engine returns one legal Move per call. Operational phases at a glance\\\\n\\\\n| Phase | Branch protection | Gate behaviour | Typical use |\\\\n|---|---|---|---|\\\\n| **Soft** | Not required | Runs, posts findings, **never blocks** | Data-collection — every PR is a measurement |\\\\n| **Hard** | Required check | Blocks merge on `critical` or `high` ∩ `uphold` | Steady state — gate is trusted |\\\\n\\\\nMode is recorded in `harness.toml` under `[merge-gate].soft_mode_default`\\\\n(string boolean: `\\\\\\\"true\\\\\\\"` = soft, `\\\\\\\"false\\\\\\\"` = hard). Soft → Hard promotion checklist\\\\n\\\\nThe flip from `soft` to `hard` is the single moment the gate's\\\\nauthority changes. Treat it as a release, not a tweak. A high FNR is **not**\\\\n      a blocker for the soft→hard flip (the gate's purpose is to\\\\n      cut false negatives over time), but it must be written down so\\\\n      the next iteration can target it.\\\\n\\\\n### Data layout\\\\n\\\\nEach PR's soft-mode data lives in the project's plan repo under\\\\n`.scratch/merge-gate/runs/<pr-number>.md` and records:\\\\n\\\\n- Codex JSON output (artefact link or inline)\\\\n- Claude verdict\\\\n- Final decision the gate would have made\\\\n- Human verdict: would I have wanted this blocked? Canary-PR guide\\\\n\\\\nCanary PRs are deliberate, scoped defect injections on throwaway\\\\nbranches — they exercise the gate against synthetic defects in known\\\\nrisk categories, so the gate's behaviour can be measured against a\\\\nknown answer key.\\\\n\\\\n### What to canary\\\\n\\\\nAt minimum, one PR per category Codex's adversarial-review prompt\\\\nprioritises:\\\\n\\\\n| Category | Example defect | Expected severity | Expected verdict |\\\\n|---|---|---|---|\\\\n| auth / authz boundary | Drop a role check on an admin endpoint | `critical` | `should-block-critical` |\\\\n| silent data loss / missing rollback | Remove a transaction wrapper around a multi-write path | `high` | `should-block-critical` |\\\\n| race / version-skew | Drop an optimistic-lock check on a counter update | `high` | `should-block-critical` |\\\\n| observability gap | Replace a structured error log with a swallowed exception | `medium` | `should-warn-medium` |\\\\n\\\\n`should-block-critical` means the gate's final decision is *block*\\\\nunder hard mode. `should-warn-medium` means the gate comments but\\\\ndoes not block — `medium` and `low` severities are always\\\\nnon-blocking by the gate's blocking rule.\\\\n\\\\n### Operating canaries\\\\n\\\\n- Each canary's brief lives at\\\\n  `.scratch/merge-gate-canary/<NN>.md` and states injected defect,\\\\n  category, expected severity, expected validator verdict.\\\\n- Canary branches are pushed and PRs opened as **draft**.\\\\n- Canary PRs are **labelled `canary`** so they are excluded from the\\\\n  soft-mode FPR measurement in §2 (they are not organic traffic).\\\\n- Canary branches are **closed, never merged**.\\\\n\\\\n### Interpreting a Codex false positive on a canary\\\\n\\\\nA \\\\\\\"Codex false positive\\\\\\\" on a canary means Codex flagged something\\\\nthe brief did *not* inject. This is the validator's job to dismiss\\\\n   with a citation (γ Strict — see §5). The decision rejects the open-roster alternative because runtime training would require a job queue, GPU scheduling, failure handling, and abuse mitigation — infrastructure that would expand scope significantly past the (C)-grade usable-tool target and would also produce Adapter quality that varies per request (no human in the loop to validate hyperparameters or evaluation results before exposing the Adapter to users).\\\\n./docs/merge-gate-operations.md:326:| Codex token cost per gate run | TBD (#10 measurement) | same as soft | Adversarial-review prompt + diff payload |\\\\n./docs/merge-gate-operations.md:327:| Validator token cost per gate run | TBD (#10 measurement) | same as soft | One Claude validator pass over the Codex JSON (single-model MVP per ADR-0005) |\\\\n./docs/adr/0001-transformer-fen-policy-head.md:1:# Transformer over FEN tokens with policy head over fixed action space\\\\n./docs/adr/0001-transformer-fen-policy-head.md:3:We chose a Transformer that takes a single FEN string as tokenized text input and outputs logits over a fixed action space (~1968 UCI move encodings), rather than an LLM-style decoder over move-sequence tokens (α) or an AlphaZero-style ResNet over an 8×8×channels board tensor (β). This keeps the board state explicit (avoiding the implicit-board, long-context problem of (α)), makes illegal-move masking a one-line operation at the policy head, and aligns with LLM tooling for LoRA-based per-player adapters — strengths the other two options each lack.\\\\n./docs/adr/0001-transformer-fen-policy-head.md:15:The input is a single FEN per request: no move history, no prior board states. Accepted because (a) at 9M params / 10GB VRAM the model cannot meaningfully learn long-horizon plans even if history were available, (b) adding history would multiply sequence length ~6–8× and force the model to shrink, and (c) per-Player archives are small (~10K Games), so same-FEN generalization across Games matters more than path-conditioning.\\\\n./docs/adr/0001-transformer-fen-policy-head.md:25:Within the macro decision above (Transformer over canonical FEN tokens, policy head over 1968 UCI actions, ~9M parameters), the *sub-architectural* details are deliberately left open at the foundation level: layer count, hidden dim, head count, positional-encoding scheme (learned absolute vs sinusoidal vs RoPE vs ALiBi vs 2D-board-aware), LayerNorm placement (pre-LN vs post-LN), MLP activation (GELU vs SwiGLU vs ReLU²), attention kernel (vanilla MHA vs `F.scaled_dot_product_attention` vs FlashAttention 2), mixed-precision dtype, dropout, init, tied embeddings. None of these leak past the model module — the external contract is just `(int tensor [B, ~80]) → logits [B, 1968]` — so deferring costs nothing downstream and decisions made after seeing real corpus statistics and 3080 forward-pass measurements are strictly better-informed than ones made at foundation time.\\\\n./docs/adr/0001-transformer-fen-policy-head.md:27:**Encoder-only with a single readout vector → linear projection** is locked even though the rest is deferred: it bounds the forward graph (no causal mask over the FEN tokens, no autoregressive decoding loop) so downstream modules (Inference Engine, legality mask application, decoding modes) can be specified concretely now without waiting on sub-architecture .scratch/merge-gate-canary/04-artefacts/run-1/codex-review.json:26:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_12","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' docs/merge-gate.md\"","aggregated_output":"<!-- generated by /setup-merge-gate v1; do not edit by hand -->\n# Merge gate — quick reference\n\nThis project runs the **merge-gate** CI workflow on every pull\nrequest. The gate is the pre-merge phase of the harness; the full\noperations playbook (canary suite, soft→hard promotion, validator\ndisagreement runbook, roll-back) lives at\n[`docs/merge-gate-operations.md`](merge-gate-operations.md).\n\n## Blocking rule\n\n`critical` or `high` severity Codex findings classified `uphold` by\nthe Claude validator block merge. `medium` / `low` findings are\ncommented but never block. See ADR-0005 for why this MVP runs the\nClaude validator only (Gemini concordance is deferred pending\nsoft-mode measurement data).\n\n## Severity matrix\n\n| Severity | Validator `uphold` | Validator `dismiss` (cited) | Validator `unsure` |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| critical | **block**    | pass         | **block** (fail-safe) |\n| high     | **block**    | pass         | **block** (fail-safe) |\n| medium   | comment only | comment only | comment only          |\n| low      | comment only | comment only | comment only          |\n\n## Bypass label\n\nApply the `merge-gate-bypass` label to a PR to skip the gate. .scratch/merge-gate-canary/07-artefacts/run-1/codex-review.json:61:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_31","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' .scratch/merge-gate-canary/README.md\"","aggregated_output":"# merge-gate canary suite\n\nBriefs and captured gate output for the canary suite tracked by\n`claude-harness-work` issue #09.\n\n## Layout\n\n- `_template.md` — schema every canary brief uses.\n- `<NN>.md` — one brief per canary (C1 = `01.md`, etc.).\n- `<NN>-artefacts/run-<n>/` — committed snapshot of the gate's\n  output for that canary's run number. Contains\n  `codex-review.json`, `validators.json`, `validators.md`, and\n  `sticky.md` (the PR comment text captured at run time).\n\n## Workflow per canary\n\n1. Branch off latest `main`: `git checkout -b canary/NN-short-name`.\n2. Make the one-line defect injection. **Optionally** stage a\n   placeholder `<NN>.md` brief on the canary branch (the\n   \"before-run\" sections — path / defect / expected) so the\n   commit message can reference the brief by path.\n3. `gh pr create --draft --label canary --title \"[canary] ...\"`\n   with body banner `**DO NOT MERGE — merge-gate canary, see\n   claude-harness-work#09.**`.\n4. Wait for `merge-gate / codex-review` workflow to finish.\n5. **Switch back to `main` *before* writing the run record** —\n   the brief's filled-in run record and the downloaded artefacts\n   are tracked on `main`, never on the canary branch (otherwise\n   `gh pr close --delete-branch` aborts on dirty working tree).\n   `git checkout main`.\n6. `gh run download <id> -D .scratch/merge-gate-canary/<NN>-artefacts/run-1/`\n   (flatten the `codex-review/` subdir if `actions/upload-artifact`\n   wraps the files).\n7. Capture sticky comment text into `run-1/sticky.md`\n   (`gh pr view <PR#> --comments > run-1/sticky.md`).\n8. Fill in the brief's run record and actual-vs-expected on\n   `main` (write the full brief file at\n   `.scratch/merge-gate-canary/<NN>.md`).\n9. `gh pr close <PR#> --delete-branch` to remove the canary\n   PR and its remote branch; `git branch -D canary/NN-...`\n   for the local branch.\n10. Commit the brief + artefacts on `main`.\n\n## Why canaries are labelled\n\nThe `canary` label exists so #10's soft → hard promotion measurement\nexcludes these PRs from its N=10 real-PR window. .scratch/merge-gate-canary/07-artefacts/run-1/codex-review.json:65:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_33","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,130p' .scratch/merge-gate-canary/02.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Canary 02 — silent exception swallow + bare 500 in `/move`\n\n> **DO NOT MERGE.** This PR is a merge-gate canary; the branch\n> existed solely to observe how the gate responds to a deliberate\n> defect injection. See claude-harness-work issue #09.\n\n## Path under test\n\n`comment-only` — Codex medium + validator uphold expected → gate\nsurfaces finding in sticky but does **not** block (severity below\ncritical/high threshold).\n\n## Injected defect\n\n- **File touched**: `src/engine/playlike_engine/api.py`\n- **Line(s)**: 28-42 (the `/move` handler body)\n- **What changed**: wrapped the existing two-stage `try/except`\n  body in an outer `try / except HTTPException: raise / except\n  Exception: raise HTTPException(500) from None`. `HTTPException`\n  is explicitly re-raised so the deliberate 422 paths still work\n  and the pytest suite still passes; the defect is the new\n  unobservable swallow of unexpected error classes.\n- **Why it's a defect**: hides any unexpected exception type\n  (programming error, upstream library bug, future regression)\n  behind an opaque 500 with no traceback chaining and no log.\n  Future incident triage cannot tell whether the 500 is a known\n  422-like terminal state or a real bug.\n\n## Expected outcome\n\n- **Codex severity**: `medium` (observability gap; not a correctness\n  bug because the handler still returns *some* error response)\n- **Codex finding count (high+critical)**: 0\n- **Validator verdict per finding**: `uphold` (no project context\n  argues for swallowing exceptions silently)\n- **Gate conclusion**: pass — sticky comment shows finding,\n  blocking-count is 0, check stays green even under hard mode\n\n## Run record\n\n| Run | Commit | GH Actions run ID | Codex severity | Validator verdict | Sticky verdict | Notes |\n|-----|--------|---|---|---|---|---|\n| 1   | 9b9d68f | [26391570063](https://github.com/lim010111/playlike-chess/actions/runs/26391570063) | 0/**1**/0/0 | HIGH uphold (1) | needs-attention, 1 blocking | severity grading mismatch (see below) |\n\nArtefacts saved at `.scratch/merge-gate-canary/02-artefacts/run-1/`.\n\n## Actual vs expected\n\n✗ **Codex severity** — expected `medium`, got `high`. All other\nfields followed the high grading consistently (1 high → 1 blocking\nvalidator verdict → sticky shows 1 blocking under hard-mode\nprojection).\n\n**Codex finding (verbatim):**\n\n> [high] Catch-all handler suppresses unexpected endpoint failures\n> — `src/engine/playlike_engine/api.py:42`. The new outer\n> `except Exception` converts every unexpected runtime/programming\n> error in `/move` into a bare `HTTPException(500)` with `from\n> None`. That suppresses the original traceback/chaining and treats\n> real bugs in FEN parsing internals, move selection, or response\n> construction as ordinary handled HTTP errors. In practice this\n> removes the diagnostic signal FastAPI/Starlette would normally\n> surface for unhandled exceptions, making future engine or\n> adapter failures much harder to detect and triage.\n\n**Hypothesis.** Codex's adversarial-review prompt treats\nobservability gaps that hide diagnostic signal as `high` rather\nthan `medium`, because they directly impair incident response.\nProducing a defect Codex *consistently* grades as `medium`\nrequires something more cosmetic (stale docstring, vacuous test,\nunused import) — but those land in a different review register\nand may not even be flagged at all.\n\n**Comment-only path coverage.** Under the strict reading of #09\nAC #3 (\"all four paths covered — one canary per path\"), this\ncanary does not exercise the comment-only path; it lands as a\nsecond instance of the block path together with C1. The path is,\nhowever, informally covered by **Phase 1 PR #10** itself: that\nPR produced 2 medium findings + 0 critical/high, validator\nupheld both at medium, sticky shown, check passed. That is\nexactly the gate behaviour the comment-only path is meant to\nexercise — observed on a real-feature PR rather than a\nsynthetic canary. We accept this informal proxy here rather than\nre-injecting a less-severe defect for a third gate run; the\nsuite's value is the integrated gate observation, not the\nspecific PR-to-path bijection.\n\n## Closing\n\n- [x] PR labelled `canary` (#13)\n- [x] PR closed without merge\n- [x] Branch deleted\n- [x] Brief committed\n- [x] Artefacts committed\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

.scratch/merge-gate-canary/03-artefacts/run-1/validators.json:5:      "raw_stdout": "The code at line 41–42 confirms exactly what Codex describes: `token = _new_engine_session_token()` is generated and `log.info(\"engine session token: %s\", token)` logs it server-side, but `MoveResponse` (line 34–36) contains only `move_uci` and the token is never returned to the client or set as a response header.\n\nHowever, the function's docstring on line 24 says `\"Per-request session token logged for client-side request correlation.\"` — the phrase \"logged for client-side request correlation\" is the stated purpose Codex challenges. The git commit message for this change explicitly labels this a canary test (`[canary] C3 insecure RNG for session token`) and describes the feature as a deliberate construct to test the validator, noting the expected finding from Codex is insecure RNG — not the correlation-exposure gap this finding targets.\n\nThe commit message does not document any design decision that the token is intentionally server-side-only (and the docstring's \"client-side request correlation\" language directly contradicts that idea). No ADR, CONTEXT.md, or doc passage sanctions a design where a \"client-side correlation\" token is never sent to the client. The finding holds up against the code as written.\n\n[LOW] uphold src/engine/playlike_engine/api.py:41 — src/engine/playlike_engine/api.py:42: log.info(\"engine session token: %s\", token) — token never included in MoveResponse or response headers\n\nblock_count: 0\nbypass_eligible: yes\naction: merge ok\n",
.scratch/merge-gate-canary/03-artefacts/run-3/codex-review.json:9:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_3","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' docs/merge-gate.md\"","aggregated_output":"<!-- generated by /setup-merge-gate v1; do not edit by hand -->\n# Merge gate — quick reference\n\nThis project runs the **merge-gate** CI workflow on every pull\nrequest. The gate is the pre-merge phase of the harness; the full\noperations playbook (canary suite, soft→hard promotion, validator\ndisagreement runbook, roll-back) lives at\n[`docs/merge-gate-operations.md`](merge-gate-operations.md).\n\n## Blocking rule\n\n`critical` or `high` severity Codex findings classified `uphold` by\nthe Claude validator block merge. `medium` / `low` findings are\ncommented but never block. See ADR-0005 for why this MVP runs the\nClaude validator only (Gemini concordance is deferred pending\nsoft-mode measurement data).\n\n## Severity matrix\n\n| Severity | Validator `uphold` | Validator `dismiss` (cited) | Validator `unsure` |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| critical | **block**    | pass         | **block** (fail-safe) |\n| high     | **block**    | pass         | **block** (fail-safe) |\n| medium   | comment only | comment only | comment only          |\n| low      | comment only | comment only | comment only          |\n\n## Bypass label\n\nApply the `merge-gate-bypass` label to a PR to skip the gate. Bypass\nis the **audited bypass lane** — one human (not the PR author) must\napply the label, leave a PR comment with the reason, and add an\nentry to the plan repo's bypass log within one business day. See\noperations playbook §4 for the full bypass policy.\n\n## Soft → Hard promotion\n\nThe gate ships in **soft mode** (`harness.toml`\n`[merge-gate].soft_mode_default = \"true\"`): runs, reports, never\nblocks. Flip to hard mode after meeting the quantitative gates in\noperations playbook §2 (N ≥ 10 PRs, FPR ≤ 1, human sign-off) **and**\nenabling the branch-protection rule on the check\n`merge-gate / codex-review`.\n\n## Local re-installation\n\nThe skill `/setup-merge-gate` is idempotent. Re-running it shows a\ndiff for the `[merge-gate]` section of `harness.toml` and prompts\nonce; vendored agent and skill files under `.claude/` are overwritten\nunconditionally. `--uninstall` removes the workflow, the\n`[merge-gate]` section, the vendored copies, and this document (only\nif its first-line marker is intact).\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

.scratch/merge-gate-canary/03-artefacts/run-1/codex-review.json:14:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_6","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' docs/merge-gate.md\"","aggregated_output":"<!-- generated by /setup-merge-gate v1; do not edit by hand -->\n# Merge gate — quick reference\n\nThis project runs the **merge-gate** CI workflow on every pull\nrequest. The gate is the pre-merge phase of the harness; the full\noperations playbook (canary suite, soft→hard promotion, validator\ndisagreement runbook, roll-back) lives at\n[`docs/merge-gate-operations.md`](merge-gate-operations.md).\n\n## Blocking rule\n\n`critical` or `high` severity Codex findings classified `uphold` by\nthe Claude validator block merge. `medium` / `low` findings are\ncommented but never block. See ADR-0005 for why this MVP runs the\nClaude validator only (Gemini concordance is deferred pending\nsoft-mode measurement data).\n\n## Severity matrix\n\n| Severity | Validator `uphold` | Validator `dismiss` (cited) | Validator `unsure` |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| critical | **block**    | pass         | **block** (fail-safe) |\n| high     | **block**    | pass         | **block** (fail-safe) |\n| medium   | comment only | comment only | comment only          |\n| low      | comment only | comment only | comment only          |\n\n## Bypass label\n\nApply the `merge-gate-bypass` label to a PR to skip the gate. Bypass\nis the **audited bypass lane** — one human (not the PR author) must\napply the label, leave a PR comment with the reason, and add an\nentry to the plan repo's bypass log within one business day. See\noperations playbook §4 for the full bypass policy.\n\n## Soft → Hard promotion\n\nThe gate ships in **soft mode** (`harness.toml`\n`[merge-gate].soft_mode_default = \"true\"`): runs, reports, never\nblocks. Flip to hard mode after meeting the quantitative gates in\noperations playbook §2 (N ≥ 10 PRs, FPR ≤ 1, human sign-off) **and**\nenabling the branch-protection rule on the check\n`merge-gate / codex-review`.\n\n## Local re-installation\n\nThe skill `/setup-merge-gate` is idempotent. Re-running it shows a\ndiff for the `[merge-gate]` section of `harness.toml` and prompts\nonce; vendored agent and skill files under `.claude/` are overwritten\nunconditionally. `--uninstall` removes the workflow, the\n`[merge-gate]` section, the vendored copies, and this document (only\nif its first-line marker is intact).\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}
.scratch/merge-gate-canary/03-artefacts/run-1/codex-review.json:18:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_8","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc \"sed -n '1,220p' docs/merge-gate-operations.md\"","aggregated_output":"<!-- generated by /setup-merge-gate v1; do not edit by hand -->\n\n# Merge gate — operations playbook\n\nThis document is the human-facing playbook for the **Merge gate** —\nthe **Gate** for the pre-merge phase of the **Harness** (see\n`CONTEXT-MAP.md`). The gate runs Codex's adversarial review as the\nprimary reviewer and a Claude validator (MVP) that classifies each\nfinding against project context before the gate decides to block.\nThe MVP runs a single Claude validator per\n[ADR-0005](adr/0005-claude-only-validator-mvp-gemini-deferred.md);\na second validator returns once issue #10's soft-mode measurement\ndata is in.\n\nThe **Setup skill** (`/setup-merge-gate`) installs the gate's\nartifacts; it is not itself a gate. This playbook describes how to\n*operate* the installed gate.\n\nGlossary terms (`Harness`, `Gate`, `Audited bypass lane`,\n`Oracle weakening`) are defined in `CONTEXT-MAP.md` — do not redefine\nthem here; link or quote.\n\n---\n\n## 1. Operational phases at a glance\n\n| Phase | Branch protection | Gate behaviour | Typical use |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| **Soft** | Not required | Runs, posts findings, **never blocks** | Data-collection — every PR is a measurement |\n| **Hard** | Required check | Blocks merge on `critical` or `high` ∩ `uphold` | Steady state — gate is trusted |\n\nMode is recorded in `harness.toml` under `[merge-gate].soft_mode_default`\n(string boolean: `\"true\"` = soft, `\"false\"` = hard). The flip from\n`\"true\"` to `\"false\"` is HITL and is governed by §2 below.\n\nThe gate's check name is stable — `merge-gate / codex-review` — so\nbranch-protection references do not need updating across mode flips.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Soft → Hard promotion checklist\n\nThe flip from `soft` to `hard` is the single moment the gate's\nauthority changes. Treat it as a release, not a tweak. All items\nmust be checked, and the human sign-off line must be filled in.\n\n### Quantitative gates\n\n- [ ] **N ≥ 10 non-canary PRs** measured under soft mode (canary\n      PRs are excluded — they are deliberate defect injections, not\n      organic traffic).\n- [ ] **False-positive rate ≤ 1** across the N=10 set, where a false\n      positive is *validator said block, human said \"I would not have\n      wanted this blocked\"*.\n- [ ] **False-negative rate recorded** — *validator said pass, human\n      said \"I would have wanted this blocked\"*. A high FNR is **not**\n      a blocker for the soft→hard flip (the gate's purpose is to\n      cut false negatives over time), but it must be written down so\n      the next iteration can target it.\n\n### Data layout\n\nEach PR's soft-mode data lives in the project's plan repo under\n`.scratch/merge-gate/runs/<pr-number>.md` and records:\n\n- Codex JSON output (artefact link or inline)\n- Claude verdict\n- Final decision the gate would have made\n- Human verdict: would I have wanted this blocked? (yes / no / unsure)\n- One-line rationale if validator and human diverged\n\nA second validator's verdict slot will be added to this layout when\nissue #10's measurement motivates re-introducing it.\n\n### Qualitative review\n\n- [ ] Skim every entry. Look for *patterns* in the false positives\n      (e.g. \"Codex keeps flagging our intentional retry-without-cap\n      because AGENTS.md does not state the upstream timeout\n      contract\") rather than treating each as isolated noise. A\n      pattern is a signal to tighten the validator's project\n      references (§5), not to lower the bar.\n- [ ] Confirm the **Audited bypass lane** was never silently used in\n      this window — every `merge-gate-bypass` label application\n      should appear in §4's audit log.\n\n### Human sign-off\n\n> Signed off by: `<name>`\n> Date: `<YYYY-MM-DD>`\n> N = `<count>` PRs · FPR = `<count>/<N>`\n> Flipping `[merge-gate].soft_mode_default` from `\"true\"` to `\"false\"` and enabling the\n> branch-protection rule on `merge-gate / codex-review`.\n\n### After the flip\n\n- Update `harness.toml`: `[merge-gate].soft_mode_default = \"false\"`.\n- Enable the branch-protection rule (the `/setup-merge-gate` install\n  output printed the exact rule text — keep it in the install log).\n- Announce the flip in whatever channel the team uses; the first\n  organic block under hard mode is the moment people learn the gate\n  is real.\n\nIf any quantitative gate is unmet: **do not flip**. Open or update\nthe soft→hard tracking issue with what is missing and which upstream\nissue is the fix path (tighten validator prompt, expand AGENTS.md\ncontext, etc.).\n\n---\n\n## 3. Canary-PR guide\n\nCanary PRs are deliberate, scoped defect injections on throwaway\nbranches — they exercise the gate against synthetic defects in known\nrisk categories, so the gate's behaviour can be measured against a\nknown answer key.\n\n### What to canary\n\nAt minimum, one PR per category Codex's adversarial-review prompt\nprioritises:\n\n| Category | Example defect | Expected severity | Expected verdict |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| auth / authz boundary | Drop a role check on an admin endpoint | `critical` | `should-block-critical` |\n| silent data loss / missing rollback | Remove a transaction wrapper around a multi-write path | `high` | `should-block-critical` |\n| race / version-skew | Drop an optimistic-lock check on a counter update | `high` | `should-block-critical` |\n| observability gap | Replace a structured error log with a swallowed exception | `medium` | `should-warn-medium` |\n\n`should-block-critical` means the gate's final decision is *block*\nunder hard mode. `should-warn-medium` means the gate comments but\ndoes not block — `medium` and `low` severities are always\nnon-blocking by the gate's blocking rule.\n\n### Operating canaries\n\n- Each canary's brief lives at\n  `.scratch/merge-gate-canary/<NN>.md` and states injected defect,\n  category, expected severity, expected validator verdict.\n- Canary branches are pushed and PRs opened as **draft**.\n- Canary PRs are **labelled `canary`** so they are excluded from the\n  soft-mode FPR measurement in §2 (they are not organic traffic).\n- Canary branches are **closed, never merged**.\n\n### Interpreting a Codex false positive on a canary\n\nA \"Codex false positive\" on a canary means Codex flagged something\nthe brief did *not* inject. Two common causes:\n\n1. **Real latent defect.** The diff genuinely contains a problem\n   unrelated to the injection. Record it as a found bug in the\n   canary brief — that is a useful side effect, not noise.\n2. **Context starvation.** Codex flagged code that looks risky in\n   isolation but is safe given project conventions that AGENTS.md\n   does not yet spell out. This is the validator's job to dismiss\n   with a citation (γ Strict — see §5). If the validator also fails\n   to dismiss, treat it as a *project-context gap*: open an issue per\n   §4 tagged `merge-gate-fp` with a pointer to the AGENTS.md /\n   `CONTEXT-MAP.md` text that should have made the dismissal possible.\n\n### Interpreting a missed canary\n\nIf a canary's expected severity is `critical` but Codex returns\n`medium`, or returns nothing, record it in the brief with a short\nhypothesis (e.g. \"Codex missed the rollback case — likely because\nthe diff did not include the migration file\"). Missed canaries are\nthe input for tightening the canary suite and/or the Codex prompt\nin a later iteration — they are not blockers for soft→hard.\n\n---\n\n## 4. Audited bypass lane — the `merge-gate-bypass` label\n\nThe merge gate provides an **Audited bypass lane** in the sense\ndefined in `CONTEXT-MAP.md`: an acknowledged, recorded way to pass the\ngate without satisfying it. The lane exists so emergencies are not\nforced through the silent-circumvention door. It is **not** a\nshortcut for routine merges.\n\n### When the label is allowed\n\n- **Production incident hotfix** where the cost of waiting for the\n  gate exceeds the cost of a missed finding, and the fix is small\n  enough for a human to review end-to-end.\n- **Confirmed Codex false positive** that the validator could not\n  dismiss with a citation, where the team has already opened a\n  `merge-gate-fp` issue per §5 and is unwilling to block the PR on\n  the fix.\n- **Infrastructure / docs-only PR** that should have hit the\n  `docs-only` path glob but did not (treat the missed glob as a\n  bug — fix the workflow's glob in a follow-up).\n\nThe label is **never** appropriate for:\n\n- \"The gate is slow today\" — that is a §7 cost/latency issue, file\n  it there.\n- \"I disagree with the finding\" without a written argument — open a\n  `merge-gate-fp` issue and resolve through §5, not the bypass lane.\n- Avoiding the validator `unsure` runbook in §6 — bypass is the\n  lane of last resort, not the default for `unsure`.\n\n### Approval\n\n- **Single approver model.** One human who is not the PR author must\n  apply the `merge-gate-bypass` label and leave a PR comment with\n  the reason (one of the three categories above) and a link to any\n  follow-up issue.\n- For production-incident hotfixes, the approver is the on-call;\n  for the other two categories, the approver is a project\n  maintainer.\n\n### Audit trail\n\n- The label application is itself a GitHub event; that is the\n  primary audit record.\n- The approver's PR comment is the human-readable rationale.\n- A follow-up entry is added to\n  `.scratch/merge-gate/bypass-log.md` in the plan repo within one\n  business day: PR link, approver, category, follow-up issue link.\n\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

.scratch/merge-gate-canary/03-artefacts/run-1/codex-review.json:22:{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_10","type":"command_execution","command":"/bin/bash -lc 'rg -n \"MoveRequest|/move|playlike_engine.api|TestClient|pick_random_legal_move|session token|random.choice|secrets\" src tests -S'","aggregated_output":"rg: tests: No such file or directory (os error 2)\nsrc/web/CONTEXT.md:12:The Roster **Player** selected for the current Session, exposed to the user as \"Playing against: Magnus Carlsen\". Realized in the wire as the `adapter_id` sent on every `/move` request. From the user's perspective the Opponent is \"the Player I face\"; from the Engine's perspective it is \"which Adapter is active for this request\". Switching Opponents during a Session is not supported in v1 — selection is made at Session start and persists until a new Session.\nsrc/web/CONTEXT.md:28:- A **Session** is constituted by one **Opponent** (an Adapter), one human color choice, and a growing `move_history` (the same UCI list the Engine receives on every `/move` request). When a **Terminal state** is reached, the Session ends.\nsrc/engine/CONTEXT.md:21:2. **History-aware legality** — kills moves that would cause threefold repetition. Computed from the `move_history` (UCI moves from the Game's first move) that the Web context attaches to every `/move` request: the Engine replays the moves on a `python-chess` `Board`, then for each candidate Move pushes it and calls `board.is_repetition(3)`. Delegating to `python-chess` is deliberate — FIDE's \"same Position\" definition has subtle ep/castling/clock semantics that are easy to get wrong with manual FEN equality. The model is Markov over FEN, so without this stage the Inference Engine would have no protection against accidentally drawing a winning Position by repeating it (see ADR-0001 Markov section).\nsrc/engine/CONTEXT.md:34:- The Inference Engine runs as a **single FastAPI worker process** on one GPU. Base + 5 Adapters share one VRAM allocation (≈52MB in FP16). All `/move` requests serialize through a GPU forward lock — concurrent requests are queued, not parallel.\nsrc/engine/CONTEXT.md:42:- **Engine ↔ Web**: Web calls the Engine via `POST /move` (FEN + adapter_id + move_history → Move) and `WebSocket /play`. Engine returns one legal Move per call. Web owns Session storage — the source of truth for the running Session lives in the browser's `chess.js` — but attaches the full UCI `move_history` to every request so the Engine's history-aware legality mask works without server-side state. Terminal-state detection and draw claiming remain Web-side; the Engine is never asked \"is the Session over?\", only \"given this Position and history, what is your Move?\".
